name: Mairee

name: The Leaf
desc: Survey Camp “The Leaf” (Site Prospect Alpha-7, Mairee Delta) is the colony’s primary high-risk outpost for foraging and water extraction, overseeing a distributed network of DROP sites that supply roughly 20% of total potable water. Embedded within the volatile Myco-Rhizomic System (MRS), the camp sits in an environment likened to a “churning petri dish,” marked by extreme humidity, sweet-rotting compost odors, and recurring psychedelic spore storms, where terrain can unpredictably liquefy or calcify. Physically, it forms a defensive “scar in the forest,” composed of hexagonal modular habitats clustered around a central command tent and enclosed by a fortified hexagonal perimeter of barricades, automated turrets, and sonic deterrents. The site operates under constant biological threat—from predatory fauna such as Shimmercats, Clackers, and Dartswarms to invasive flora including microscopic Burr-vines and reactive Gramophone Plants—resulting in a persistently unstable operational state where overstretched personnel maintain only a fragile equilibrium against an ecosystem actively attempting to reclaim the installation.
mj_prompt: An alien jungle of impossible density, enormous bioluminescent plants with razor-edged leaves, gramophone-shaped flowers tilted toward the light, mist threading between root systems taller than buildings, everything faintly pulsing, cinematic 16:9, photorealistic, anamorphic lens, shallow depth of field, dramatic chiaroscuro lighting, desaturated teal-and-amber palette, gritty sci-fi atmosphere, film grain, ultra-detailed, --ar 16:9 --style raw --v 7

name: Muspel
desc: The closest planet to Yra. A scorched, iron-rich world. Temperatures high enough to melt lead on its dayside. No atmosphere worth speaking of — what little gas clings to its surface is metallic vapour. Geologically violent and entirely inhospitable. Nobody has proposed a use for it yet.

name: Ysk
desc: Third planet of the Yreus system. A cold desert with a thin carbon dioxide atmosphere and polar ice caps of frozen water. Surface temperatures average -60°C. Possible future outpost — the ice caps suggest subsurface liquid water may have existed, and may exist still. It would be a difficult one. Everything here would be.

name: The Ginnunga Gap
desc: A wide, dense asteroid belt beyond Ysk — far larger and more concentrated than Sol's own. Named for the mythological void of Norse cosmology. Rich in silicates, iron-nickel, and volatile ices. Navigation through it is technically possible but practically dangerous without good sensor coverage. The Muninn and Hugin would have been useful here.

name: Ymir
desc: The larger of Yreus's two gas giants. Dark and majestic, with deep charcoal-grey banding and a system of rings — thin and dark, absorbing rather than reflecting light. Ymir has the silent authority of something enormous and indifferent. Its gravity well is a navigational hazard for anything on a low-fuel trajectory inbound from the Fold.

name: Nefir
desc: The outermost planet of the Yreus system. A smaller ice giant that glows deep blue in the distant twilight — methane-rich upper atmosphere scattering short wavelengths back into the void. Cold enough to freeze nitrogen. Barely visible from Mairee with the naked eye. It looks like a star that never quite made it.

name: Skuld
desc: The largest of Mairee's three moons. Named for the Norn of the future. Tidally locked, heavily cratered on its far side. Its gravitational interaction with Mairee is thought to contribute to the planet's unusually stable axial tilt — one of the reasons Mairee was hospitable enough to colonise in the first place.

name: Verdandi
desc: The middle moon of Mairee. Named for the Norn of the present. Smaller and faster than Skuld, completing its orbit in just over a Mairee day. From the surface, it rises and sets twice in a single click. Some colonists track it compulsively — there is something about its speed that feels like time passing faster than it should.

name: Urdra
desc: The smallest and most distant of Mairee's three moons. Named for the Norn of the past. Barely more than a captured asteroid — irregular, dark, with a retrograde orbit suggesting it arrived from the Ginnunga Gap. It rarely appears in the same part of the sky as Skuld or Verdandi. It keeps its own schedule.

name: Kepler Array
desc: A deep-space communications relay positioned near the Sol mouth of the Zaytsev Fold to maximize signal range across the transit gap.
